title: Warm up
content: This slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrations of taxi drivers navigating each city's roads. Each image reflects a distinct urban environment and driving experience, hinting at different city characteristics.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with modern skyscrapers, traditional architecture, and heavy traffic with a dashcam visible.
Chongqing is shown with a driver reading a book amid mountainous, layered architecture and a cable car.
Hangzhou features a driver using a smartphone app with AI/robot graphics overlaid, near a scenic lake.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This title slide introduces the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using imagery of a taxi sign alongside iconic Chinese architectural and cultural landmarks. It sets up the metaphor of exploring China's development through the perspective of a taxi ride.
key_claims: The presentation is framed as 'A Ride Through China'.
Imagery includes a taxi sign, the Forbidden City, illuminated traditional buildings, and a pagoda tower.

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1, presenting a three-step process for investigating city development. An isometric city illustration accompanies a flowchart showing the logical progression of analysis.
key_claims: The investigation process follows three steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact.
Each step flows sequentially into the next, as shown by arrows.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This is an interactive matching activity where students connect three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their corresponding development strategies. The matches are shown with red connecting lines, with supporting images of each city's architecture on the right.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with 'Tourism & Economic growth'.
Chongqing is matched with 'Sustainable urban development'.
Hangzhou is matched with 'Technology & tradition integration (融合)'.

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide synthesizes the taxi driver imagery from the warm-up with the correct development strategies for each city, revising the earlier matching activity. It concludes with the overarching theme that China's cities tailor development strategies to their unique local characteristics.
key_claims: Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜).
